text: The surgeon reduces the mesentery of the right colon and the kidney from the hernia defect, achieving complete reduction of the hernia contents.
The surgeon incises the peritoneum and creates a preperitoneal flap proximal to the medial aspect of the hernia defect, followed by the reduction of the hernia sac.
The posterior aspect of the hernia defect is visible, and the surgeon closes the defect primarily using an OV-lock suture.
The surgeon begins closing the posterior aspect of the hernia defect to offload tension over its widest portion, while reducing pneumoperitoneum to eight millimeters of mercury.

The surgeon completes the re-approximation of the transversus and internal oblique muscles. A 20x25 cm sheet of physio mesh is placed to reinforce the repair, secured to the abdominal wall with secure strap tackers. The peritoneal flap is re-approximated using tackers, with no mesh exposed.
